prompt: This reflective field access throws IllegalAccessException. What is the bug?
choices: getDeclaredField only works on public fields
f.setAccessible(true) must be called before accessing a private field reflectively
int fields cannot be retrieved with f.get() — use f.getInt() instead
The Secret class must be public for its fields to be accessible via reflection
explanation: getDeclaredField finds the private field successfully, but the JVM still enforces the access modifier when get() is called. f.setAccessible(true) suppresses that check for this Field object. Add it between getDeclaredField and get(): f.setAccessible(true); System.out.println(f.get(s));
